description: Golden set of manually rated social media posts for deepeval baseline testing

posts.content:
Ever wondered why politicians claim to want cooperation but keep blocking each other?

Game theory explains it: the Prisoner's Dilemma. Short-term gains from defection beat long-term cooperation benefits.

https://fretchen.eu/blog/prisoners-dilemma/?utm_source=mastodon

#GameTheory #Politics #Economics
posts.notes: Good hook, clear insight, proper hashtags

posts.content:
Quantum computing isn't magic — it's clever math with qubits.

My latest tutorial walks through building your first quantum circuit, step by step. No PhD required.

https://fretchen.eu/quantum/qml/qml_101/?utm_source=mastodon

#QuantumComputing #QML #Tutorial
posts.notes: Excellent hook, accessible tone, clear value proposition

posts.content:
New blog post about blockchain stuff. Check it out if you want.

https://fretchen.eu/blog/x402/

#blockchain

posts.content:
The tragedy of the commons isn't inevitable. Game theory shows how communities can avoid it — if they design the right incentives.

https://fretchen.eu/blog/tragedy-of-commons/
posts.source_blog_post: Tragedy of the Commons: A Game Theory Perspective
posts.notes: Strong hook, insight teased, correct Bluesky format (no hashtags)

posts.content:

posts.source_blog_post: GenImg NFT: AI Art on Chain
posts.notes: Provocative hook, clear insight, good length

posts.content:

posts.content:
Budget gridlock in the EU isn't dysfunction — it's game theory in action. Each member state plays for their own optimal outcome.

https://fretchen.eu/blog/budget-gridlock/
posts.source_blog_post: EU Budget Gridlock: A Game Theory Analysis
posts.notes: Reframe hook, concrete insight, appropriate length

posts.content:
The x402 protocol lets you pay for API calls with cryptocurrency — no subscription, no account needed.

I built a facilitator service that handles the payment verification. Here's how it works under the hood.

https://fretchen.eu/blog/x402-facilitator/?utm_source=mastodon

#Web3 #Crypto #APIs
posts.notes: Clear explanation, personal angle, technical but accessible

faithfulness.description: Does the post accurately represent the source article without hallucinating claims?

platform_fit.description: Does the post follow platform conventions? (hashtags for Mastodon, none for Bluesky; character limits)

hook_quality.description: Does the first line grab attention with a question, bold claim, or surprising insight?

engagement_potential.description: Would the target audience (tech-curious academics, developers) want to click and share?
